title: Two-Colour-Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
context_text: The Two-Colour-Conjecture was posed by Erdős and Neumann-Lara and independently by Skrekovski as a directed analogue of the Four-Colour-Theorem. The strongest prior partial result (Mohar and Li) established 2-colourability for oriented planar digraphs of girth at least 4. The paper's main theorem (Theorem 1.10) yields a proof of the conjecture for the non-trivial intersection of non-even digraphs with planar oriented digraphs.
paper_contribution: Proves that every non-even digraph has dichromatic number at most 2, which settles Conjecture 1.1 for all planar oriented digraphs that are non-even.
